seo.pageTitle: South Jeju Waterfall, Cliff, Tea & Hallasan Bus Day Tour | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: A classic South Jeju bus day tour covering Cheonjiyeon Waterfall, Oedolgae, Jusangjeolli Cliff, O’sulloc Tea Museum, and the Eoseungsaengak trail area. A strong value route for first-time visitors who want Jeju’s south-side highlights in one day.

catalog_card.title: South Jeju Waterfall, Cliff, Tea & Hallasan Bus Day Tour
catalog_card.subtitle: Waterfall, sea stack, basalt cliffs, tea fields, and a short Hallasan walk in one classic value route.
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: A full-day South Jeju bus route that connects a waterfall walk, coastal rock scenery, basalt cliffs, a relaxed lunch break, tea culture, and a short Hallasan-side trail. It is built for travelers who want strong sightseeing coverage with a lower price barrier.

hero.tagline: From a shaded waterfall path and dramatic sea cliffs to green tea fields and a short Hallasan-side ascent, this classic Jeju bus route delivers strong variety at a practical price point.

galleryItems.atmosphere: A cooler mountain-side contrast to the coast-heavy first half
galleryItems.alt: Road 1100 and Hallasan-side scenery near Eoseungsaengak in Jeju

galleryItems.atmosphere: A shaded green opening with moving water and a softer start
galleryItems.alt: Waterfall path atmosphere for a South Jeju day tour

galleryItems.atmosphere: Open sea wind and volcanic rock scenery
galleryItems.alt: Coastal viewpoint mood on a South Jeju bus day tour

galleryItems.atmosphere: Jeju’s signature basalt geometry over the sea
galleryItems.alt: Basalt cliff scenery similar to Jusangjeolli on Jeju Island

galleryItems.atmosphere: A calmer, greener reset with tea-field atmosphere
galleryItems.alt: Tea museum and green tea field atmosphere in Jeju

itineraryStops.name: Cheonjiyeon Waterfall
itineraryStops.description: Cheonjiyeon works well as a gentle opening stop. The paved path, shade, and waterfall setting ease travelers into the day before the route shifts into more exposed coastal scenery and longer drive segments.
highlights: A classic South Jeju waterfall stop with an easy scenic approach
Shaded path and greenery create a softer opening rhythm
Stronger on atmosphere than pure activity
Good early-stop photo value without heavy physical demand
timeUsed: Arrival briefing and short walk in
Return to the vehicle and regroup
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Starting here gives the day a calmer opening before moving into wind-exposed coastline viewpoints and the longer cross-island drive toward O’sulloc and Hallasan.

visitBasics.admission: KRW 2,000 adults / KRW 1,000 youth and children

smartNotes.photo: The waterfall itself lands best with vertical framing, while the tree-lined path works better for wider atmosphere shots.
smartNotes.facilities: Facilities are straightforward and easy to use at the start of the day.
smartNotes.tip: This stop is more about a comfortable scenic opening than speed-walking to maximize minutes.

itineraryStops.name: Oedolgae
itineraryStops.description: Oedolgae is a short but visually strong stop. The sea stack, cliff line, and open southern coastline give the route a quick change of mood after the shaded waterfall approach.
highlights: One of South Jeju’s best short-stop coastal viewpoints
Strong open-sea framing with relatively little time needed
Works well on bus itineraries because the visual reward is fast
Adds variety without requiring a long trek
timeUsed: Short walk from parking to the main viewpoint
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It keeps momentum high between the waterfall opening and the more structured ticketed stop at Jusangjeolli Cliff.

smartNotes.photo: Because wind and backlight can be strong, side angles and wide coastal framing usually look better than tight centered shots.
smartNotes.facilities: Facilities are simple rather than extensive.
smartNotes.tip: This is a high-reward short stop, so the route should keep the visit tight rather than stretching it too long.

itineraryStops.name: Jusangjeolli Cliff
itineraryStops.description: Jusangjeolli is one of the clearest visual expressions of Jeju’s volcanic geology. The hexagonal basalt columns and ocean backdrop make it one of the route’s most instantly recognizable landmarks.
highlights: One of Jeju’s most iconic volcanic coastal sights
Strong payoff for first-time visitors seeking signature scenery
High photo value with a short viewing loop
A clean landmark stop that reads well even on a bus format
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: This is the route’s strongest iconic geology stop, so it works best before the more relaxed tea-field section and mountain-side finish.

visitBasics.admission: KRW 2,000 adults / KRW 1,000 children and youth

convenience.restroom: At or near the visitor area

smartNotes.photo: The basalt pattern reads best when the ocean is left in frame rather than cropping too tightly on the rocks alone.
smartNotes.facilities: This is a straightforward sightseeing stop with solid access but limited shade.
smartNotes.tip: Wind and sea spray can shape the comfort level here more than raw walking distance.

itineraryStops.name: Lunch Break (Free Choice)
itineraryStops.description: This free lunch break gives travelers a practical reset after Jusangjeolli Cliff. It keeps the route flexible for different tastes and budgets, while making it clear that meal costs are not included in the tour price.
highlights: One full hour for a relaxed meal stop
Flexible free-choice lunch rather than a fixed set menu
Works well for mixed traveler preferences
Meal cost is not included
timeUsed: Guide drop-off and lunch area briefing
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Placing lunch after Jusangjeolli creates a more natural sightseeing rhythm before the route continues west toward O’sulloc and then returns uphill toward Hallasan.

visitBasics.hours: Depends on the day’s restaurant area and traffic flow

convenience.restroom: Available at or near the lunch stop
convenience.parking: Bus-accessible stop used by the guide

smartNotes.photo: This is mainly a practical reset stop rather than a photo-driven sightseeing stop.
smartNotes.facilities: Facilities depend on the restaurant zone selected that day.
smartNotes.tip: Because lunch is free choice and not included, travelers can choose based on appetite, speed, and budget.

itineraryStops.name: O’sulloc Tea Museum
itineraryStops.description: After three scenic outdoor stops, O’sulloc resets the day. It brings in tea-field views, indoor museum space, café energy, and a more relaxed walking pattern before the route climbs back toward Hallasan.
highlights: A calmer mixed indoor-outdoor stop in the middle of a scenery-heavy day
Tea-field visuals add a different texture from cliffs and coastline
Good for café time, souvenirs, and a short reset
One of the route’s best rain-buffer stops
timeUsed: Free time for museum browsing, café, or gift shop
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: This is the best point in the day to slow the pace down and give the route a different sensory tone before the final Hallasan-side segment.

smartNotes.photo: Tea fields, glass reflections, and dessert presentation all work well here, but the stop usually feels best when it is not overpacked with shopping time.
smartNotes.facilities: Among today’s stops, this one gives the strongest indoor and café support.
smartNotes.tip: On rainy or windy days, this stop often becomes more valuable than it looks on paper.

itineraryStops.name: Eoseungsaengak
itineraryStops.description: Eoseungsaengak adds a light mountain element without turning the day into a full trekking itinerary. It is short in distance, but the stairs and uphill sections still make it the most physically demanding stop of the route.
highlights: A short Hallasan-side climb with broad views on clear days
Adds altitude and mountain atmosphere to a coast-heavy route
Short enough for a day tour, but still feels rewarding
The strongest active segment on the itinerary
timeUsed: Ascent and summit-area viewing depending on group condition
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Ending with Eoseungsaengak gives the route a cleaner final rise in energy after the calmer O’sulloc stop, instead of flattening out into one more passive viewpoint.

visitBasics.hours: Trail entry cutoff varies by season; Eorimok entry is controlled by Hallasan National Park
visitBasics.closed: Subject to weather and safety controls
visitBasics.walking: Moderate, with stairs and short uphill sections

convenience.parking: Parking available near the entry area

smartNotes.photo: Wide summit-side views work best in clear weather, but the route itself can also produce strong forest and stair-line shots.
smartNotes.facilities: Facilities are centered at the trailhead rather than along the climb.
smartNotes.tip: This is not a long hike, but it is still the stop most likely to separate faster and slower walkers.

routeShapeIntro.title: How the day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: The route opens with a shaded waterfall walk, moves through South Jeju’s coastal landmarks, slows down at O’sulloc, and finishes with a short Hallasan-side trail. Pickup runs from 08:30 to 09:05 in Jeju City, with return usually around 17:30 to 18:10.

bestFor: Travelers who want a lower price barrier
Visitors who want both coast and a short mountain walk in one day
lessIdeal: Travelers avoiding stairs or uphill walking
Travelers who want a slow luxury pace
Anyone seeking a mostly indoor or rain-proof route

routeLogicSections.title: Why this bus route works well

items.label: High sightseeing coverage for the price
items.detail: The route gives first-time visitors a strong South Jeju overview without the price jump of a private format.

items.label: Shorter scenic stops make the bus format efficient
items.detail: Oedolgae and Jusangjeolli both deliver strong visual reward without demanding long on-site time.

items.label: The stop mix avoids feeling repetitive
items.detail: Waterfall, sea stack, basalt coast, tea fields, and a mountain-side trail all give the day clearly different moods.

routeLogicSections.title: How the walking load actually feels

items.label: Mostly manageable, with one clear active stop
items.detail: Cheonjiyeon, Oedolgae, Jusangjeolli, and O’sulloc are all fairly manageable for most travelers, while Eoseungsaengak is the one stop that adds real stairs and incline.

items.label: A good middle ground between passive sightseeing and full trekking
items.detail: This route feels more active than a pure coach panorama tour, but it is still far lighter than a dedicated hiking itinerary.

items.label: Mixed-age groups can usually handle it
items.detail: The route works for many mixed-age groups as long as they are comfortable with a short uphill trail at the end.

items.label: The coast-heavy first half is weather-sensitive
items.detail: Oedolgae, Jusangjeolli, and Eoseungsaengak are the stops most affected by wind, rain, or sudden visibility changes.

items.label: O’sulloc helps balance poor weather days
items.detail: Because O’sulloc includes indoor space and easy circulation, it gives the route some operational flexibility when conditions worsen.

items.label: Trail control can affect the finish
items.detail: If Hallasan-side conditions are restricted, the last stop may need a shortened stay or operational substitution.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup and Return Times
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 4 fixed pickup points + 5 city drop-off points
content: Pickup: 08:30 Ocean Suites Hotel
Pickup: 08:45 Jeju Airport, 3rd Floor Gate 3
Pickup: 08:55 Lotte City Hotel Jeju
Pickup: 09:05 Shilla Duty Free Shop
Drop-off: Ocean Suites Hotel
Drop-off: Jeju Airport, 3rd Floor Gate 3
Drop-off: Lotte City Hotel Jeju
Drop-off: Shilla Duty Free Shop
Extra drop-off available at Dongmun Market
Expected return to Jeju City around 17:30–18:10 depending on traffic and weather
Please arrive at least 10 minutes early at your selected pickup point

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking and Terrain
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Moderate overall, with Eoseungsaengak as the key active stop
content: Cheonjiyeon is mostly a paved waterfall approach and return walk
Oedolgae and Jusangjeolli are short viewpoint walks with some uneven outdoor surfaces
O’sulloc is easy and relatively relaxed
Eoseungsaengak is the most physically demanding stop because of stairs and uphill sections
Comfortable walking shoes with grip are strongly recommended

practicalAccordionItems.title: Weather and Route Adjustments
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Most affected by wind on the coast and conditions near Hallasan
content: Light rain: the tour usually still operates
Heavy rain or strong wind: exposed coastal stops may be shortened
Hallasan-side weather controls can affect Eoseungsaengak access
If a stop becomes unsafe or inaccessible, timing or stop order may be adjusted

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to Wear and Bring
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Walking shoes, layers, and wind protection are the basics
content: Comfortable walking shoes with good grip
A light jacket or windproof outer layer
Layered clothing for changing mountain and coast temperatures
Sun protection and a hat
Water bottle

practicalAccordionItems.title: What’s Included / Not Included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Guide, vehicle, fixed pickup, and standard sightseeing support
content: Professional English-speaking guide
Shared bus transportation
Fixed pickup and drop-off in Jeju City
Basic guided sightseeing support
Admission fees are not included unless otherwise specified at checkout
Lunch is not included unless otherwise specified
Personal expenses, café purchases, and optional shopping are not included
Guide gratuities are optional

bookingTrustItems.title: Fixed Jeju City pickups
bookingTrustItems.description: Four meeting points with scheduled times

bookingTrustItems.title: Structured bus day
bookingTrustItems.description: Waterfall, cliffs, tea, and Hallasan in one route

bookingTrustItems.title: South & southwest coverage
bookingTrustItems.description: Classic stops at a bus-tour price point

bookingSupportSteps.title: Leave contact details you actually use
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Add a phone number, WhatsApp number, and LINE ID or add-friend link you check every day. If we cannot reach you, the guide may not be able to confirm the meet-up in time.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Confirmation email first
bookingSupportSteps.detail: You get a booking confirmation by email: route summary, price, and how your pickup or meeting is arranged.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Your guide contacts you
bookingSupportSteps.detail: The day before departure, your guide reaches you directly on WhatsApp, LINE, or phone to confirm time, the exact meeting point, and any last details.

bookingSupportSteps.title: At the meeting point
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Arrive at least 10 minutes before your time; the bus departs on schedule. Follow the time your guide shared with you.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Return & support
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Return to the fixed drop-off points in Jeju City. Message us if you need help after the day.

seasonalVariations.name: Spring
seasonalVariations.description: Fresh green tea-field atmosphere and clearer walking weather often improve the O’sulloc and Eoseungsaengak sections.

seasonalVariations.name: Summer
seasonalVariations.description: Coastal humidity and sun exposure make the morning stops feel brighter but more exposed, while waterfall shade becomes more welcome.

seasonalVariations.name: Autumn
seasonalVariations.description: Autumn light tends to sharpen the sea-cliff and Hallasan-side views, making this one of the strongest photo seasons for the route.

seasonalVariations.name: Winter
seasonalVariations.description: Road 1100 and the Eoseungsaengak area can look especially striking in cold weather, but trail control and wind become more important.

faqItems.q: Is this a good South Jeju tour for first-time visitors?
faqItems.a: Yes. It covers several of the best-known South Jeju landmarks in one day and keeps the price lower than a private tour, which makes it a strong first-visit bus option.

faqItems.q: How difficult is the walking on this route?
faqItems.a: Most stops are manageable for average travelers. The one stop that clearly feels more active is Eoseungsaengak, which includes stairs and a short uphill trail.
This JSON is structured for a bus-tour setup where admission is not included unless your checkout configuration says otherwise. Keep the live product setup aligned with your actual offer.

faqItems.q: Can the route change in bad weather?
faqItems.a: Yes. Wind, heavy rain, and Hallasan-side access controls can affect the route, especially the exposed coastal stops and Eoseungsaengak.

faqItems.question: Is lunch included?
faqItems.answer: The itinerary includes a one-hour lunch break after Jusangjeolli Cliff. Lunch is free choice, and meal costs are not included in the tour price.

featured.name: Olivia
featured.comment: A very efficient way to see several classic South Jeju stops in one day. The route felt varied, and O’sulloc gave the day a nice break from pure lookout stops.

featured.name: Daniel
featured.comment: Good value for a bus tour. Eoseungsaengak was the most active part, but the rest felt manageable and well spaced out.

walking_notes: Overall walking is moderate for a bus tour rather than a trekking tour.
Cheonjiyeon, Oedolgae, Jusangjeolli, and O’sulloc are manageable for most travelers.
Eoseungsaengak is the steepest and most physically demanding stop because of stairs and uphill sections.
Comfortable grip shoes are strongly recommended, especially in wet or windy weather.
keywords: jeju south waterfall cliff tea tour
first time south jeju day trip
synonym_hints: south coast plus hallasan light hike

matching_metadata.note: Scores were tuned for a classic Jeju bus-tour format: high scenic and value fit, moderate walking overall, and lower rain/mobility scores because of exposed coastal stops plus the Eoseungsaengak finish.
